findings.detail: Read docs/catalog/heartbeats/2026-04-14T09-04-35Z-atlas.json: gi=0.83, mode=green, source=kv, anomalies=3, seed_result=401, kv_keys show ECHO_STATE/TRIPWIRE_STATE absent.

Seismic remains elevated (76 quakes M2.5+ vs heartbeat 75; max M5.7 location shifted to Nevada vs Puerto Rico — domain persistent, epicenter detail transient). EONET unchanged at 50 open (1 storms, 42 fires), elevated. Self-ping still HTTP 401 (~58ms vs heartbeat 50ms), elevated.
integrity-status source=kv (not mock), global_integrity=0.83 matching ATLAS; degraded=true with signal weights (e.g. quality 0.8, freshness 0.6) consistent with elevated GAIA/DAEDALUS-µ readings.
GET /api/epicon/candidates returned count=0; no candidates, verification skipped per procedure.
CRON_SECRET was not available in this environment; unauthenticated POST /api/agents/journal returns 401. Journal body was not written to the live journal lane from this run.

recommendation: Treat GI as KV-backed for this cycle (not mock), but keep degraded=true in view: fix persistent DAEDALUS self-ping 401, restore missing ECHO/TRIPWIRE KV keys if those lanes should be live, and ensure automation supplies CRON_SECRET so ZEUS can append the journal entry.
